clauses.text: This Agreement is entered into between [CLIENT_NAME] ("Client") and [PROVIDER_NAME] ("Service Provider").
This Agreement governs the provision of [PROJECT_NAME] by the Service Provider to the Client.
The Service Provider shall produce the content described in [SERVICES_CONTRACTED] for [PROJECT_NAME], covering [CONTENT_PIECES_PER_PERIOD] pieces per [BILLING_PERIOD].
The Service Provider shall deliver the Deliverables to the Client on or before [DELIVERY_DATE].
The Services shall take place on [DELIVERY_DATE] at , subject to any change confirmed in writing by both Parties.
The Client shall pay the Service Provider [TOTAL_VALUE] for the Services, payable via [PAYMENT_METHOD] according to the schedule agreed between the Parties.
If the Client fails to pay any amount due by its due date, the Service Provider may charge interest on the overdue amount at [LATE_PAYMENT_RATE] per month.
The Service Provider retains copyright in the Deliverables. Upon full payment of [TOTAL_VALUE], the Client receives a [LICENSE_SCOPE] license to use the Deliverables for [PERMITTED_USE].
Where the Client requests translation of a content piece, the Service Provider shall provide it in [TRANSLATION_LANGUAGES] for an additional fee of [TRANSLATION_FEE] per language per piece.
Where the Services include graphic design elements accompanying the content, the Service Provider shall provide them for an additional fee of [GRAPHIC_DESIGN_FEE] per piece.

Where the Client requests publishing to [PUBLISHING_PLATFORMS], the Service Provider shall publish the content there on the Client's behalf. The Client shall be responsible for compliance with each platform's own terms of service regarding the published content.

The Service Provider shall propose topics for upcoming content in [EDITORIAL_CALENDAR_REFERENCE]. The Client shall approve each topic within [TOPIC_APPROVAL_PERIOD_DAYS] days before drafting begins. If the Client does not respond within this period, the topic shall be deemed approved.
The Client may request up to [INCLUDED_REVISION_ROUNDS] rounds of revisions per piece at no additional charge. Additional rounds shall be charged at [ADDITIONAL_REVISION_FEE] per round.
The Client shall review each content piece within [APPROVAL_PERIOD_DAYS] days of delivery. If the Client does not respond within this period, the piece shall be deemed approved.

Each Party shall keep confidential any non-public information disclosed by the other Party in connection with this Agreement and shall not disclose it without the disclosing Party's prior written consent.

Neither Party shall be liable for a failure to perform its obligations to the extent that failure results from an event beyond that Party's reasonable control that could not have been avoided through reasonable diligence. The affected Party shall notify the other Party without undue delay.
This Agreement shall be governed by [APPLICABLE_LAW]. Any dispute shall be submitted to [DISPUTE_RESOLUTION_MECHANISM] before litigation, except where interim injunctive relief is required.

The Parties have executed this Agreement as of [SIGNATURE_DATE]. Ambiguity: none — Editorial Calendar requires explicit topic approval before drafting begins, with a deemed-approval fallback, preventing wasted work on unapproved topics while avoiding indefinite delay. Duplication: Additional Revisions vs. Approval Process kept distinct — the former caps iteration rounds, the latter governs the delivery review/deemed-acceptance timeline, mirroring the pattern established across Design and Videography Markets. Loophole closed: Publishing Clause explicitly shifts platform-terms-of-service compliance responsibility to the Client, closing a gap where the Provider could be blamed for a third-party platform's independent policy issues. Contradiction: none. Missing protection: Copyright Clause reused verbatim from the Market-wide pattern (Provider retains copyright, Client receives license upon payment), ensuring consistency with Design and Photography Markets' treatment of the same underlying legal concept. Complexity: kept Translations and Graphic Design as separate per-piece priced add-ons, avoiding a single bundled and ambiguous "extras" fee. Enforceability: topic approval and revision caps both explicit Dynamic Fields. Attack: Provider drafts an entire piece on a topic the Client never approved, then bills for it — mitigated by Editorial Calendar's mandatory topic-approval-before-drafting requirement. Attack: Client demands unlimited rewrites on a single article, treating "revisions" as unbounded — mitigated by Additional Revisions' explicit per-piece cap. Attack: Content published to a third-party platform violates that platform's content policy, and the platform penalizes the Client's account, who then blames the Provider — mitigated by Publishing Clause's explicit allocation of platform-compliance responsibility to the Client. No further gaps found. Version 1.0.0 — Self-Review and Red Team passed — Pending Human Legal Review.
